usage_note: ENT clinics operate primarily on an appointment schedule with stochastic patient punctuality. The IEEE paper's central research question (context row 2) is specifically about alternative appointment schedules.
Patients differentiated by visit type (new patient, follow-up, referral) and possibly acuity or insurance class.

Residents (multiple experience levels), attending physicians, and nursing staff have different service speeds and capabilities — the Illinois ENT study tested resident allocation with a 5.29% wait time reduction from adding one resident.
Clinic sessions have fixed start and end times with resources operating on session-based schedules.
